title: Americas: States intensify crackdown on civil society through βÄ€anti-NGO lawsβÄù
content:
Several countries across the Americas are adopting restrictive legal frameworks to weaken, control, and even dismantle civil society organizations, Amnesty International said today as it launched its new report Tearing Up the Social Fabric: Impact of restrictive laws on civil society organizations in the Americas.

Between 2024 and 2025, Ecuador, El Salvador, Nicaragua, Paraguay, Peru and Venezuela adopted or reformed legal frameworks that impose disproportionate controls on civil society organizations (CSOs), directly affecting their ability to support communities, defend human rights, operate, and access resources.

title: Earth being βÄ‰pushed beyond its limitsβÄô as energy imbalance reaches record high
content:
State of the Climate report finds EarthβÄôs energy has moved dangerously out of balance, with oceans absorbing vast majority of trapped heat

Our home planet is struggling with a record energy imbalance, which is warming oceans to unprecedented levels, making weather more extreme and threatening health and food supplies, the World Meteorological Organization has warned.

The United Nations body confirmed 2015 to 2025 were the hottest 11 years ever measured, but a still bleaker message was that the rising temperature experienced by humans on the surface was only 1% of the faster-accumulating heat in the wider Earth system.

title: When your culture becomes a meme: the βÄ‰jarringβÄô effect of Chinamaxxing
content:

I have been Chinese my whole life. Lately, many online have also found their Chinese roots, but not through traditional ancestry tests.

Creators are drinking hot water, wearing slippers around the house, using chopsticks, eating Chinese food, and wearing red. Taking off in popularity from mid-2025, these videos have racked up hundreds of thousands of views, finding virality first on TikTok, then later Instagram and X. title: Central Asian experts visit Finland and Norway to explore Nordic regional cooperation
content:
A group of experts from Central Asian countries visited Helsinki and Oslo in February. The delegation met and exchanged with a range of Nordic experts with the objective to deepen their understanding of Nordic cooperation in various areas of policy. At a time of growing multipolarity and global instability, the visit was part of CMIβÄôs engagement in Central Asia and sought to foster the exchange of ideas and best practices across regions.

CMI has engaged in the broader region since 2022 and sees Central Asian countries finding themselves at the crossroads of great power politics, seeking to define a more common foreign policy identity and role within both the region and the emerging global order. title: Trump Has Detained the Parents of More Than 11,000 U.S. Citizen Kids
content:
The baby needed somewhere to go. So in the frantic hours before officers took her parents away to immigration detention, her mom turned to their pastor and his wife. As squad cars waited outside the familyβÄôs Lakeland, Florida, trailer home, she gave them a crash course in how to care for the 4-month-old.

Briany, with her plump cheeks and full head of dark hair, wasnβÄôt normally this fussy. But it was late that January night βÄî around midnight βÄî and she was still hungry. Her mom, Doris Flores, had tried nursing her to calm her down. It didnβÄôt work. When she brought Briany to her breast, the milk wouldnβÄôt come. Flores thought it had to do with the panic that set in after the officers arrested the babyβÄôs father and told her she was next.

The baby also drank formula.
creator: Jeff Ernsthausen
sourceDescription: Latest Articles and Investigations from ProPublica, an independent, non-profit newsroom that produces investigative journalism in the public interest.

title: Using βÄ€Data AgreementsβÄù in universities to clarify research data rights of use
content:
<html><head></head><body>"The Earth, Space, and Environmental Sciences are research disciplines in which a large amount of research data is generated and in which the principles of FAIR and open data are now receiving considerable attention.

Both FAIR and open data aim to enable and enhance the reusability of data, but before research data can be made available for broad reuse, it is essential to clarify rights and permissions: who is authorized to share the data and with whom, who may publish it, how credit for data-related work will be attributed, and what arrangements apply if a researcher transfers to another institution.

Concrete regulation of usage rights for research data continues to pose major challenges for researchers and research institutions alike. There are legal uncertainties due to room for inter</body></html>

title: Democracy Decided by Algorithm: How Romania Became the First Country to Cancel Elections Because of TikTok
content:
Romania is still trying to understand how it set a precedent that many analysts consider dangerous. The cancellation of the 2024 presidential elections, due to manipulation through TikTok, marked the defeat of democracy in the face of social networks, a Romanian researcher argues in a study published in March.

Credited in polls with only a few percentage points, CΡÉlin Georgescu came first in the first round of the presidential elections on November 24, 2024. A public figure previously considered marginal, Georgescu quickly transformed into a candidate supported by an overwhelming wave of messages on social media, which portrayed him almost as a messianic figure on TikTok, Facebook, YouTube, and other platforms.

Behind this

What?

Open licensing and copyright are important aspects of publishing research, but what do they mean in practice? Creative Commons (CC) licences offer a standardised framework for sharing research outputs, with open access being increasingly mandated by international funders. title: Competition, Innovation, and the Future of the Web βÄ™ Why Independent Browser Engines Matter
content:
Gecko matters because it ensures thereβÄôs an independent voice shaping how the internet evolves. Without Gecko, the landscape would be dominated by Apple and Google alone.

From accessing information, communicating with others, shopping, working, learning, and entertainment, the vast majority of our time online is spent within a browser. While there are many browsers out there, there are only a few browser engines, the technology necessary to render the data that makes up the web as websites we can use.

βÄùToissavuonna lauantaina tila meni aivan tukkoon. title: Stuit | How Graduate Students Engage with Open Access: Challenges and Interventions | Journal of Librarianship and Scholarly Communication
content:
<html><head></head><body>Abstract: Introduction: As graduate students learn to publish their research, they also must navigate the complex, increasingly open access (OA) landscape of publishing. This study investigates graduate studentsβÄô engagement with OA at an R1 university.

Literature Review: Literature reveals that graduate students are strategic about maximizing research impact but face knowledge gaps from informal learning, financial constraints that vary by discipline, and limited agency in publishing decisions. While students show positive attitudes toward OA, practice lags, suggesting they need strategic guidance on integrating OA into impact-focused publishing plans rather than general awareness-building.

Methods: Graduate students (N = 245) at the University of California, Santa Cruz were survey</body></html>

title: Poisoned Trekkers and Phantom Flights: Nepal Charges 32 in Massive Himalayan Rescue Scam
content:
For years, the towering peaks of the Himalayas have drawn thousands of thrill-seekers to Nepal. But according to prosecutors, many of those tourists were led into a carefully orchestrated trap: secretly fed baking soda by their own guides to induce severe illness, only to be βÄ€rescuedβÄù in a highly lucrative insurance fraud scheme.

Following a sprawling, months-long police investigation, authorities filed organized crime and fraud charges against 32 individuals in the Kathmandu District Court. The case implicates a deep network of prominent tourism stakeholders, including trekking agency owners, helicopter operators, and hospital executives.

Nine suspects were produced in court on Sunday, while 23 others remain at large.

title: From Overwhelming Lists to Bite-Sized Edits: Meet the Microtask Generator
content:
Introduction

Imagine youβÄôre organizing a Wikimedia campaign. Your community has spent months identifying the knowledge gaps that matter most in your language. YouβÄôve created a list of articles that need improvement. You have motivated editors who genuinely want to help. Then someone asks the simple but difficult question: βÄ€Where do we start?βÄù If you tell a newcomer, βÄ€Here are 1,000 articles. Go improve them,βÄù it can feel overwhelming. That is not a clear task. It is just a very long list with no obvious first step.

A campaign needs the same kind of clarity. Not just a big goal, but a clear starting point. When people know what they are being asked to do, they are much more likely to contribute and to come back! That is exac

title: Couples share 30% of their gut bacteria. HereβÄôs how that may affect health
content:
When living with a partner, you might be sharing more than just the same home, lifestyle and interests. You might also share various microscopic organisms residing on and in you.

This community of microorganisms, which consists of mainly bacteria, viruses and fungi, is known collectively as the human microbiome. The various microbiomes found throughout the body all play an important role in health.

title: βÄ‰Kids say they take a quick look at TikTokβÄô: a new kind of distracted driving is on the rise
content:
As watching videos, using touchscreens, and even livestreaming behind the wheel become more common, experts warn of increased risk of crashes

Jackie was on her way to a doctorβÄôs appointment last fall when she realized her Uber driverβÄôs eyes were not fully on the road. βÄ€He had a video playing on his phone and was intermittently looking at it,βÄù she said. Jackie, who is 32 and lives in New Jersey, could not tell exactly what the driver was watching, but she remembers seeing shots of people talking βÄ™ she guessed it was a video podcast. βÄ€I was definitely feeling a lot of dread and distress.βÄù

As they continued on their 40-minute drive down the New Jersey Turnpike βÄ™ a hectic highway that is not easy driving βÄ™ Jackie considered saying something. But she felt vulnerable as a rider.

title: Kyrgyzstan Releases Prominent Investigative Journalist Pending Retrial
content:
A court in Kyrgyzstan released prominent investigative journalist Makhabat Tazhibek kyzy from custody on Monday, placing her under travel restrictions pending a new trial after she spent 18 months behind bars.

The decision by the Lenin District Court followed a ruling earlier this month by the Kyrgyz Supreme Court, which overturned the journalist's six-year prison sentence and ordered a retrial.

Bolot Temirov, her husband and the editor-in-chief of the investigative outlet Temirov Live, confirmed her release on Facebook.

title: We Know You Can Pay a Million by Anja Shortland review βÄ™ the terrifying new world of ransomware
content:
Criminals extorting money online have created huge businesses, complete with branding and HR

The birth of ransomware was a stunt that got out of hand. In 1989, an evolutionary biologist called Joseph L Popp Jr was working part time for the World Health Organisation on the Aids epidemic. He was a difficult man. When he was denied a permanent job, he decided to punish his peers while shocking them into acknowledging another kind of infection: the computer virus.

Popp wrote a questionnaire promising to help minimise the risk of contracting HIV, duplicated it on to 20,000 floppy discs, and sent them to researchers in 90 countries. Each disc contained a Trojan virus. Once it was inserted, a malware timebomb eventually made the computer unusable until the user paid a βÄ€licence feeβÄù
creator: Dorian Lynskey
sourceDescription: Technology news, comment and analysis | guardian.co.uk

title: Alva NoΟΪn Yhteenkietoutuneet pureutuu taiteen, filosofian ja elΟΛmΟΛn erottamattomuuteen
content:
Taide pysΟΛyttΟΛΟΛ tottumuksen, vΟΛittΟΛΟΛ filosofi Alva NoΟΪ. Se saa meidΟΛt epΟΛilemΟΛΟΛn nΟΛkemΟΛΟΛmme ja horjahtamaan kesken tutun liikkeen. Filosofia saa aikaan jotain samankaltaista. NΟΛin taide ja filosofia muuttavat toimintaamme ja osallistuvat ihmislajin kulttuurievoluutioon.

title: Help Map Diamond Open Access in the United States
content: <html><head></head><body>"Thank you for participating in this survey. This study is a national effort to map diamond open access publishing in the United States. Diamond open access journals make research freely available to readers without charging authors fees to publish. These journals are typically supported by academic institutions, libraries, scholarly societies, or other non-commercial organizations. They do not require subscriptions or payments to read or publish. This survey is intentionally brief. Our goal is to hear from as many journals as possible in order to understand the scope and diversity of diamond open access publishing in the United States. We are not asking for detailed operational or financial information at this stage. If you are willing, we may follow up for a more in-depth conversation. T</body></html>

title: Morgan le Fay was King ArthurβÄôs sister βÄ™ but also a healer, mathematician and murderer
content:
Morgan le Fay is one of the most infamous characters of Arthurian mythology. A powerful sorceress and, in later stories, King ArthurβÄôs half-sister, Morgan was a healer, a mathematician, murderer, adulteress and queen.

In later versions of the legends, Morgan is shown most often as the lover or enemy βÄ™ and sometimes both βÄ™ of many of ArthurβÄôs closest allies, including Sir Lancelot and the powerful wizard Merlin.

Her surname, le Fay, is thought to be a combination of the French and Gaelic words for fairy, and refers to her fantastical powers.

Modern versions of ArthurβÄôs story, such as the BBC program Merlin (2008βÄ™12) or the Irish/Canadian series Camelot (2011), continue this trend. They pair Morgan with Mordred, the knight who kills Arthur, pitting the two of the
creator: Nicole Kimball, Casual Academic, School of Humanities, Creative Industries and Social Sciences, University of Newcastle

title: Open science practices in behavioral addictions: An exploratory survey in: Journal of Behavioral AddictionsOnline First
content:
<html><head></head><body>Abstract: Background


The field of behavioral addictions (BA) research addresses activity domains such as excessive gaming, gambling, and other online behaviors that influence public health policies. A failure to embrace open science practices may lead to concerns about the trustworthiness and reliability of its research outputs. This study explored the current use of open science practices among BA researchers, focusing on the adoption, underlying motivations, concerns, and support needs across seven specific open science practices.



Methods

title: Open Access as a Launchpad: Educational Pathways in Early-Career Scientific Publishing | Cureus
content: <html><head></head><body>Abstract: Open access publishing has transformed the scientific dissemination landscape by expanding accessibility and accelerating knowledge exchange. Beyond democratizing readership, it may also serve as a formative platform for early-career researchers navigating the complexities of academic authorship and scholarly communication. High rejection rates, prolonged review timelines, and limited mentorship exposure often position traditional publishing pathways as barriers to entry for young investigators. In contrast, engagement with open access journals can facilitate experiential learning in manuscript development, peer-review processes, and editorial interaction. Participation in accessible publication environments may support the acquisition of technical competencies, authorship </body></html>

title: All Things Open: Free to Read, Free to Publish: Library Publishing and Global Scholarly Communication
content:
<html><head></head><body>Abstract: Open access is a right exercised by scholars and their institutions, not a privilege granted by publishers. From this perspective, library publishing is not peripheral to the open access movement but a necessary extension, and when libraries take on publishing roles, they reclaim functions long outsourced to commercial actors and re-center scholarly communication within academic communities. The partnership among libraries, institutions, and researchers is foundational to the production, evaluation, and sharing of knowledge. Library publishing shows that open access can be sustainable and scholar-driven without reliance on paywalls, article processing charges, or exclusivity.

Since 2014, library publishing has evolved from isolated local initiatives into a global prac</body></html>

title: Sylvie Njobati: βÄ€The campaign #BringBackNgonnso isnβÄôt just for my grandfather, it is for my people tooβÄù

In 2018, Sylvie NjobatiβÄôs grandfather told her about a sacred ancient artefact known as Ngonnso, stolen by the Germans during the colonial period from 1884 to 1916, and donated to BerlinβÄôs Ethnological Museum, by German Military Officer, Kurt Von Pavel.

Ngonnso was seen as a cultural cornerstone for the Nso people, an ethnic group from the Northwest Region of Cameroon, embodying their history and identity.

Keen to see it returned to its rightful home during his lifetime, SylvieβÄôs grandfather asked her if she could try and bring it back to Cameroon.

Determined to make her grandfather proud,

title: Thousands of people are selling their identities to train AI βÄ™ but at what cost?
content:
Gig AI trainers worldwide are selling moments of their lives, including calls and texts, to AI companies for quick cash

One morning last year, Jacobus Louw set out on his daily neighborhood walk to feed the seagulls he finds along the way. Except this time, he recorded several videos of his feet and the view as he walked on the pavement. The video earned him $14, about 10 times the countryβÄôs minimum wage, or for Louw, a 27-year-old based in Cape Town, South Africa, half a weekβÄôs worth of groceries.

The video was for an βÄ€Urban NavigationβÄù task Louw found on Kled AI, an app that pays contributors for uploading their data, such as videos and photos, to train artificial intelligence models. In a couple of weeks, Louw made $50 by uploading pictures and videos of his everyday life

title: βÄ‰ItβÄôs stupidβÄô: why western carmakersβÄô retreat from electric risks dooming them to irrelevance
content:
Iran war should be wake-up call about costs of not going full throttle towards EVs as Chinese have done, experts say

By the 1980s, DetroitβÄôs once titanic carmakers were being upended by rivals from Japan. Ford, General Motors and Chrysler had grown rich selling gas guzzlers, but when oil prices rose and suddenly cheap, fuel-efficient Japanese models looked attractive, they were unprepared. The collapse in sales led to hundreds of thousands of job losses in the automotive heartland of the US.

Now western car manufacturers are making what one former boss calls a similar βÄ€profound strategic mistakeβÄù as they pull back from electric vehicles (EVs) and refocus on the combustion engine just as oil prices are soaring once again. Experts say the industryβÄôs future βÄ™ and that of tens o

title: Diamond Open Access: The AMR Experiment | The Mathematical Intelligencer | Springer Nature Link
content:
<html><head></head><body>"Creating a βÄ€realβÄù journal from scratchβÄîeven one that exists only onlineβÄîrequires solving many small but nontrivial problems: DOIs, ISSNs, editorial management systems, hosting, indexing, archiving, and long-term continuity. Each component is manageable in isolation, but assembling them all without an existing template is daunting enough to deter most editorial boards from trying.


In the fall of 2021, the fledgling Association for Mathematical ResearchFootnote1 (AMR) contacted me. Its founders were interested in whether the technologies mathematicians had grown accustomed to during the pandemic might change how mathematics is practiced and communicated. This provided a golden opportunity to attack the problem I had been thinking about for so long.



Over the next year, I interviewe</body></html>

title: Water Security Demands a Systems Change and Programmatic Approach
content:
Bapon Fakhruddin, PhD., Climate Investment Principal, Green Climate Fund, Republic of Korea & Chair, CODATA TG FAIR DRR, France has published a piece in βÄôThe Water DiplomatβÄô.

Everyone now knows that the climate crisis is a water crisis. As global temperatures rise, the worldβÄôs water cycle is thrown into chaos. Drought and flood extremes are intensifying, water scarcity is spreading, and unsafe water and sanitation gaps persist on a staggering scale. Nearly 2.1 billion people lack access to safe drinking water and 3.5 billion lack basic sanitation, mostly in the poorest communities. This creates cascading and compounding impacts that steadily erode all life and livelihoods. However, global investment remains woefully inadequate. Achieving Sustainable Development Go

title: Digital transformation in academic libraries: implementing integrated research information systems for enhanced scholarly communication and institutional knowledge management | Digital Library Perspectives | Emerald Publishing
content:
<html><head></head><body>Abstract: This study aims to examine the digital transformation of an interdisciplinary Ukrainian university through the implementation of a Current Research Information System (CRIS) under conditions of economic, staffing and administrative challenges using the case of Sumy State University.

 
Design/methodology/approach

This study employs a case study methodology focusing on the Scientific Results Management System (SRMS) developed at Sumy State University as a digital solution for universities. The research combines a systematic literature review of international CRIS development practices with a technical analysis of SRMS architecture. The methodology evaluates 18 unified scholarly metrics based on international standards and assesses the operational efficiency of the SRMS</body></html>

title: Tuore tutkimus: Digitalisaatio helpottaa ulkosuomalaisten yhteydenpitoa Suomeen, mutta hallinnolliset esteet vaikeuttavat arkea
content:
Siirtolaisuusinstituutin toteuttama laaja kyselytutkimus kartoitti ulkosuomalaisten elΟΛmΟΛntilannetta ja tarpeita. Tulokset paljastavat erityisesti sosiaalisen median yllΟΛpitΟΛvΟΛn ulkosuomalaisten yhteyksiΟΛ Suomeen. SisΟΛministeriΟΕn rahoittamaan tutkimukseen vastasi 4 901 ulkosuomalaista ympΟΛri maailmaa. Raportti on nyt julkaistu verkossa ja sen julkaisutilaisuus jΟΛrjestetΟΛΟΛn HelsingissΟΛ torstaina 26.3.

Suomen rajojen ulkopuolella asuu noin 300 000 Suomen kansalaista. Kun mukaan lasketaan suomalaista syntyperΟΛΟΛ olevat henkilΟΕt, mΟΛΟΛrΟΛn on arvioitu nousevan jopa kahteen miljoonaan. Vuosittain Suomen kansalaisia muuttaa ulkomaille keskimΟΛΟΛrin 9 000βÄ™10 000 henkeΟΛ.

Nyt julkaistava Muuttuva ulkosuomalaisuus 2 -tutkimusraportti tarjoaa ajankohtaisen kuvan tΟΛmΟΛn merkit

title: Dynamiittia ja todistusaineistoa

Toukokuussa 2024 mie sain puhelun Kirjastoseuran toiminnanjohtaja Juhalta ja hetken verran luulin, ettΟΛ oon saanut seurasta jostain syystΟΛ kenkΟΛΟΛ. Edelleenkii ajattelen, ettΟΛ sekin kuulostaa paljon todennΟΛkΟΕisemmΟΛltΟΛ kuin tulla valituksi vuoden kirjastokehittΟΛjΟΛksi.

Mutta niin vaan miut valittiin. Mie olen kirjastobisneksessΟΛ kokenut ihan mielettΟΕmiΟΛ onnenhetkiΟΛ, mutta vielΟΛkin meinaa olla roska silmΟΛssΟΛ kΟΕ tuota hetkeΟΛ miettii.

Isoin hyΟΕty palkinnosta on henkisellΟΛ puolella. Mie kirjoitan tΟΛtΟΛ postausta tyΟΕpisteellΟΛni, jota somistaa kaikenlaista tyΟΕhΟΕn kannustavaa. Kummilasten kuvia, kielipoliisin haalarimerkki. title: As Above, So Online :3
content: My guardian angel has never replied to me but my computer surely didβÄ™it has always been there for us. To it we entrust our happiness, success and sorrow, we confide our deepest secrets and we seek the answers we fear to voice elsewhere. It offers us the space we need to connect with thy neighbor and allows both proximity and anonymity while hiding behind a made-up usernameβÄ™haunted still by the shame of our trespassing. During a time of widespread uncertainty, highlighted by collective feelings of dread and pessimism, we often seek answers and reassurance in the most unusual places. In moments when technology dictated all aspects of life, the digital realm takes the role of a confidant, guide and source of meaning. title: From Hitler to βÄ‰PinocchioβÄô: GermanyβÄôs speech laws collide with satire
content:
When German historian Rainer Zitelmann reposted a photo of Adolf Hitler to warn against appeasing Russian President Vladimir Putin, he didnβÄôt expect it to trigger a police probe.

According to police, the problem was the image itself: Hitler was shown wearing a swastika armband βÄî a banned symbol under GermanyβÄôs criminal code, which prohibits the public display of Nazi and other extremist insignia. Zitelmann was informed in February that authorities were examining the case.

ZitelmannβÄôs is just one of several recent investigations into online speech, which have raised questions about how far German authorities are going in enforcing strict speech laws βÄî and whether efforts to curb extremism are colliding with satire and political criticism.

title: The Number of Families Being Held at Dilley Detention Center Has Plummeted
content:
The number of parents and children booked into the countryβÄôs only immigrant family detention center, in Dilley, Texas, plummeted in February by more than 75% compared with a month earlier, according to Immigration and Customs Enforcement data obtained by ProPublica.

Between April 2025, when President Donald Trump started sending families there, and January of this year, the number of people sent into detention with their families averaged around 600 per month. In February, those so-called books-ins fell to 133. As of mid-March, they dropped again to just 54.

This week there were only around 100 people in family detention at Dilley, compared with an average daily population in January of over 900, the data shows.

title: Alleged South American Kingpin Denied Bail After Extradition to U.S.
content:
In his first public court appearance since his recent extradition from Bolivia, accused South American drug lord SebastiΟΓn Marset was ordered detained without bail by a federal judge in Virginia at a detention hearing on Friday.

Dressed in a dark green prison jumpsuit and black sneakers, the tattoo-covered Uruguayan national did not speak during the proceeding at the Albert V. Bryan United States Courthouse.

Magistrate judge William B. Porter denied bail to Marset, agreeing with the view of prosecutors that he represented a flight risk.

Marset, 34, was arrested in the early morning of March 13 in a residential neighborhood of Santa Cruz, Bolivia and extradited to the U.S. hours later.

title: The myth of meritocracy: why universal water access is a prerequisite for a fair society
content:
In developed economies, water has become a victim of its own social success. For those with universal access to it, a form of cognitive dissonance has taken hold: we fret over looming threats of scarcity while enjoying an uninterrupted, completely dependable supply. Just turn on the tap, and the problem evaporates βÄ™ along with the idea that water is inextricably linked to equality of opportunity.

For many in the Global North, water scarcity is at best a humanitarian concern, and at worst an abstract, geographically distant problem with little grounding in reality. But this sense of security is misleading. Water has

title: How active have IranβÄôs proxy groups been since the start of the war?
content:
One of the most notable aspects of the war in Iran so far has been the extent of TehranβÄôs isolation in the region. This has been exemplified not only by the widening divide between Iran and its Gulf Arab neighbours, but also by the highly variable responses to the conflict by IranβÄôs proxy groups.

Iran has relied on a network of proxies to protect and bolster its position in the region since the earliest days of the Islamic Republic in 1979. The most important elements in this network have been Hezbollah in Lebanon, Hamas in Gaza, Syria under the rule of the Assad family, Iran-aligned militias in Iraq and the Houthis in Yemen.

However, this network

title: Equal Opportunities in Europe? A Growing Skepticism Among the Young
content:
Talent, hard work or family wealth? What really determines success today? Young Europeans grew up believing they lived on a continent of equal opportunity. Today, they increasingly feel that success depends not on skills or effort, but on their parentsβÄô financial resources.

Europe is often portrayed as a continent of social fairness βÄ™ a place where prosperity, universal public education, and integration projects were meant to level the playing field. Programs such as Erasmus+, free or low-cost higher education in many countries, and extensive welfare systems were all designed with this goal in mind.

In theory, these mechanisms were supposed to ensure that a young European βÄ™ regardless of where they were bornβÄîwould have a similar start

title: Climate resilience and risk management in the Baltic Sea Region context
content: Climate change impacts in the Baltic Sea Region are already clearly visible, and extensive adaptation work is underway, particularly in larger cities. Policy Areas Secure and Nutri gathered policy messages from ongoing projects funded by the Interreg Baltic Sea Region programme and drafted a joint statement for the EUβÄôs open consultation on a new European climate resilience framework. The consultation sought to establish a more ambitious, comprehensive and coherent EU approach to climate resilience and preparedness, covering both individual Member States and the EU as a whole. Although the framework is intended for the entire EU, it is important to highlight the challenges and experiences emerging from macroregional and local levels.

title: UkraineβÄôs World-Beating Combat Library Opens for Business
content:
UkraineβÄôs friends will be allowed to train artificial intelligence systems on real battlefield datasets gathered during the war with Russia, the Ministry of Defense in Kyiv said in early March.

Companies will receive controlled access to data specifically tailored for their needs, while the full battlefield database will remain under the control of the Ukrainian government.

Ukraine, which has millions of annotated images collected during tens of thousands of combat flights, is the first country to make such a move, the ministry said, describing it as a potentially beneficial partnership.

title: Clashes in Chabahar Prison Following Protest Over Food Ration Cuts; More Than Ten Prisoners Injured
content:
HRANA βÄ™ On Wednesday, March 18, clashes broke out in Chabahar Prison after inmates protested the cut in food rations and poor detention conditions. The confrontation between prisoners and special forces resulted in more than ten inmates being injured.

Based on the report by Haal Vsh, the protest began on the evening of Tuesday, March 17, following several days of food ration cuts. During the protest, a number of prisoners gathered in the prison yard and refused to return to their wards, objecting to detention conditions, lack of facilities, and the unsafe environment caused by recent developments in Chabahar. The inmates demanded transfer to prisons with safer conditions or clarification of their legal status.

title: DOGE Goes Nuclear: How Trump Invited Silicon Valley Into AmericaβÄôs Nuclear Power Regulator
content:
Last summer, a group of officials from the Department of Energy gathered at the Idaho National Laboratory, a sprawling 890-square-mile complex in the eastern desert of Idaho where the U.S. government built its first rudimentary nuclear power plant in 1951 and continues to test cutting-edge technology.

On the agenda that day: the future of nuclear energy in the Trump era. The meeting was convened by 31-year-old lawyer Seth Cohen. Just five years out of law school, Cohen brought no significant experience in nuclear law or policy; he had just entered government through Elon MuskβÄôs Department of Government Efficiency team.

As Cohen led the group through a technical conversation about licensing nuclear reactor designs, he repeatedly downplayed health and safety concerns.
creator: Avi Asher-Schapiro

title: What the war in Iran means for Ukraine and Russia
content:
A war with Iran could influence the course of the conflict in Ukraine. Rising oil prices, limited weapons supplies, and shifting Western priorities may simultaneously strengthen Russia while complicating KyivβÄôs position.

At first glance, a conflict between Iran and the United States and their allies in the Middle East should have little impact on the war that has been ongoing in Ukraine since 2022. The two are separated by thousands of kilometers, shaped by different strategic realities and driven by distinct political objectives. However, in an era of global military, energy, and financial interdependence, any major conflict acts like a tectonic shock βÄ™ shifting the balance of power even in regions far removed from the battlefield.

title: The Value of a Europe-Friendly US Congress
content:
Allies and partners across the Atlantic should note the widening gap between the US executive and legislative branches on European security. Despite some highly critical rhetoric from the administration, Congress holds the purse strings and has consistently demonstrated a keen understanding of how the US benefits from the North Atlantic alliance.

That will only be underlined by the PresidentβÄôs delayed Fiscal Year 2027 Budget Request, which is expected soon. What finally emerges is rarely what the White House intended (and the media reported).

creator: Daniel Gaffin

βÄ€While the release of hundreds of individuals unjustly detained on politically motivated charges is a welcome step, it must not be mistaken for justice. Marfa Rabkova, Nasta Loika, Valiantsin Stefanovich and others should not have spent a day in prison. Freedom should never be the product of geopolitical bargaining in human beings. title: As Trump Demands Voter Data, This Fiercely Independent Red State Says No

title: Argentina 50 years on from start of dictatorship βÄ™ is it forgetting the disappeared?

There had been five previous coups in Argentina between 1930 and 1966. But the regime that came to power in 1976, calling itself the βÄ€process of national reorganisationβÄù, stood out for its systematic campaign of political violence and terror until the end of its rule in 1983.

title: More renovation and construction, less bureaucracy βÄ™ thatβÄôs how the EU will tackle the housing crisis
content:
Unspent funds from the Recovery plan could be redirected toward affordable housing. However, there are concerns that lowering standards will only benefit the industry.

Mobilizing funds and other financial resources to boost housing construction and the renovation of aging and energy-inefficient buildings, along with relaxing construction regulations βÄ™ these are the main measures outlined by the European Parliament after more than a year of analysis and discussion by the Special Committee on the Housing Crisis (HOUS). It tackles an issue that has become a problem for millions of Europeans: housing is scarce and unaffordable. Ultimately, it has proposed a solution, though one that has so far been met with mixed reactions.

From global fintech companies to instant payment services, Ireland has built one of EuropeβÄôs most dynamic digital finance sectors.

No company illustrates this success better than Stripe. Founded by Irish brothers Patrick and John Collison, Stripe has become one of the most important financial infrastructure companies in the global digital economy. Millions of businesses rely on its software to accept online payments.

title: She Was in Labor at a Florida Hospital. Then She Was in Zoom Court for Refusing a C-Section.
content:
ItβÄôs difficult to put yourself in the place of Cherise Doyley, a pregnant mother of three who found herself facing a judge while in labor at the University of Florida Health hospital in downtown Jacksonville.

She had arrived at the facility with a plan for her birth. She wanted to try for a vaginal delivery, but she understood from years of experience as a professional birthing doula that things donβÄôt always go as planned.

She arrived overnight at the hospital after her water broke. Doctors told her they were concerned about the risk of uterine rupture, a potentially deadly complication for her and her baby. title: Switching to open access is a long bet: longitudinal causal effects in a single-institution natural experiment | Research Square
content: <html><head></head><body>Abstract: This study estimates the causal effects of switching to open access (OA) within a single university, treating an institutional excellence initiative as a natural experiment that sharply altered local costs, support, and contractual constraints for OA publishing. To reduce self-selection bias, a strict βÄ€switcherβÄù identification strategy was applied: switchers are researchers with low baseline OA adoption before the policy shift who subsequently exhibit sustained high OA publishing behavior, while controls remain consistently low throughout. title: Kuinka edustavia ovat 1900-luvun alun perukirjat?
content: Merikarjalan rannikon elΟΛmΟΛΟΛ 1800-luvun lopulla ja 1900-luvun alussa kΟΛsittelevΟΛΟΛ vΟΛitΟΕskirjatutkimusta tehdessΟΛni olen mieltynyt perukirjoihin historiantutkimuksen lΟΛhteenΟΛ. Ne avaavat mainion ikkunan siihen materiaaliseen todellisuuteen, joka entisinΟΛ aikoina on vallinnut. Minua on kiinnostanut perukirjoihin merkityn kalastukseen, merenkulkuun ja maatalouteen liittyneen omaisuuden laatu ja mΟΛΟΛrΟΛ. Tutkimustani varten olen kerΟΛnnyt noin 600 perukirjaa kΟΛsittΟΛvΟΛn aineiston, johon olen ottanut kaikki Virolahden, SΟΛkkijΟΛrven, Koiviston ja Terijoen rannikkokylistΟΛ sekΟΛ Suomenlahden ulkosaarista vuosilta 1876βÄ™1889 ja 1909βÄ™1912 sΟΛilyneet perukirjat, sekΟΛ tietyissΟΛ tapauksissa edellΟΛ mainittujen vuosien ympΟΛriltΟΛ, joissa on ollut kalastukseen, merenkulkuun tai maatalouteen liittyvΟΛΟΛ omaisuutta
creator: Sakari Salokannel

title: Fear and Fragility: The Glass Delusion and Its History
content: In early modern Europe, around the time when lenses began to bring the world (and heavens) into newfound focus, patients started appearing in medical records with a particular ailment: a firm belief that they were made of glass. Tamara Sanderson investigates the source and manifestation of this delusion, and finds a psychological idiom that once carried the weight of what could otherwise not be said.

title: Cloud infrastructure as a foundation for digital sovereignty
content:
EuropeβÄôs dependence on a handful of US-based cloud providers is not a technical accident. It reflects deliberate market choices, policy gaps, and decades of institutional inertia. Three hyperscalersβÄîAmazon Web Services, Microsoft Azure, and Google CloudβÄînow control most of the European cloud market, while European providersβÄô combined share has fallen to around 13%.

Cloud infrastructure has become as foundational as energy or telecommunications. Yet, unlike those sectors, it lacks a regulatory framework that addresses market concentration, mandates interoperability, or ensures fair access. Governments, civil society organizations, and critical public services across Europe rely on computing capacity that is owned, operated, and ultimately controlled by actors outside European juri

title: Fighting for fragile freedoms, online and off
content:
Arzu Geybulla became Co-Executive Director at Access Now in January 2026.

Growing up, my world was shaped by the scarcity of the post-Soviet years and the rigid expectations of a patriarchal society. I was born and raised in Baku, Azerbaijan, a country marked by decades of systemic repression and persistent human rights violations. Witnessing my country consolidating into an authoritarian state gave me an early and sobering education about the cost of unchecked power. title: EUβÄôs Huawei hardliners get top court backing
content:
EU efforts to ban Huawei from 5G networks won the backing of a top court adviser Thursday, in a legal opinion that is likely to galvanize security hawks seeking to restrict Chinese tech in Europe.

A lawyer for the EUβÄôs top court in Luxembourg said rules blocking telecom operators from using risky suppliers can be set by the EU, not just national governments. They also said telecom operators donβÄôt need to be compensated for the cost of replacing Huawei equipment.

ItβÄôs a blow for EuropeβÄôs telecom giants, which have pushed back against banning ChinaβÄôs Huawei from 5G procurement and have told EU officials that large-scale bans are an βÄ€act of self-harmβÄù that could even bring down networks.

title: How Robert F. Kennedy Jr.βÄôs Vaccine Agenda Risks a Resurgence of Deadly Childhood Plagues
content:
Dr. Adam Ratner hovered over a gravely ill infant in a New York City intensive care unit on a grim day in 2022. The 3-month-old girl spiked a fever two days earlier and had become lethargic. Soon she was having seizures and struggling to breathe.

She didnβÄôt register RatnerβÄôs towering frame or the bright hospital lights. Her eyes stared up and to the right, eerily frozen.

He ran his hand over the soft spot on her head, which should have been flat. Instead, it bulged, a sign that too much fluid was building up inside her skull.

The babyβÄôs life was in danger, and Ratner needed to figure out why. He worried the culprit was bacterial meningitis, an infection of the membranes that protect the brain.

title: Before Waging War, Consult Historians First
content:
From the time of Thucydides and the Peloponnesian Wars through the advent of World War I to Vietnam, Afghanistan, Iraq and now Iran, historians have had a better grasp of the confounding crosscurrents and unintended consequences likely to result from armed conflicts than the over-confident leaders who prosecute them.

In a recent essay in The Free Press titled βÄ€This Is How The Iran War Goes Global,βÄù the conservative historian Niall Ferguson recounts once again the lessons of βÄ€applied historyβÄù that decision-makers in Washington would be wise to heed now that they have leapt headlong into the fog of war.

title: Women and girls bearing brunt of water shortages globally, UN warns
content:
Unesco calls for action as lack of access and sanitation hit health, education and food security of women

Women and girls are bearing the brunt of water shortages and a lack of sanitation around the world, hindering the economic and social development of poorer countries, the UN has warned.

Women are responsible for collecting water in more than 70% of rural households that do not have access to mains water across the developing world. Women and girls collectively spend 250m hours a day collecting water globally.

title: In the killer world of online gaming, there are no hits any more βÄ™ just survivors
content:
The fates of two ostensibly similar online games released this year, Marathon and Highguard, prove that success is becoming close to unattainable

What does success look like for developers of online video games? In 2026, the answer could not be clearer: no one has a clue.

Consider Highguard, 2026βÄôs first big flop. Signs were promising on its launch on 26 January, with a peak of 100,000 concurrent players on Steam βÄ™ plus those enjoying the game on PlayStation and Xbox, which do not make player counts public. As a free-to-play game, the barrier to entry for Highguard was low. And thanks to a prime advertising placement at the end of DecemberβÄôs The Game Awards βÄ™ a buzzy spot usually reserved for known hitmakers, not free-to-play upstarts βÄ™ curiosity was high.
creator: Joshua Rivera

title: Highlights from the Wikimedia EndowmentβÄôs fiscal year 2024-2025 audit report
content: The Wikimedia Endowment is a permanent fund designed to generate income in perpetuity for the Wikimedia projects, safeguarding their future for generations to come. Since 2023, the Endowment has operated as a standalone nonprofit, working independently from the Wikimedia Foundation but toward the same free knowledge mission. As its own 501(c)(3), the Endowment now issues its own annual financial statements. This post highlights key insights from the EndowmentβÄôs audited financial statements (βÄ€audit reportβÄù) for fiscal years 2024 and 2025, covering the period from July 1, 2024 through June 30, 2025. It provides context behind the numbers and explains what they mean, offering donors and community members a clearer understanding of how the fund is performing and evolving over time.
creator: Elena Lappen

Operation Epic Fury posed that question in real terms βÄî and Europe had no good answer. In practice, European countries chose the first two of the above, and unity vanished.

title: Profiting From Atrocities: The Russian Civilians Running a Lucrative Market for War Snuff
content:
Russian Telegram channels dedicated to broadcasting graphic war footage from UkraineβÄîincluding apparent executions and the mutilation of corpsesβÄîhave morphed into a highly profitable online industry operated in part by civilians and teenagers, according to a new investigation.

The independent Russian news outlet IStories analyzed more than 50 channels focused on war snuff. The investigation revealed that administrators draw tens of thousands of followers, monetizing extreme battlefield violence through advertising, cross-promotion, direct donations, and paid access to uncensored material.

Visual spectacle overwhelms the human drama in the choreographerβÄôs tech-heavy double bill

Technology can sometimes seem to take on its own life and sideline the people it is nominally assisting. That tension, even conflict, is the subject of Mirror, a new duet by Alexander Whitley, who has good form with choreographic deployments of digital, generative and VR technologies.

title: A New Russian Game on the Borders of the Baltics
content:
A small Telegram channel has begun in recent weeks to promote the idea of a so-called βÄ€Narva PeopleβÄôs RepublicβÄù in EstoniaβÄôs northeastern border city of Narva, where almost all the inhabitants are Russian speakers.

The campaign uses separatist slogans, meme-style content, and imagery that imitates the aesthetics of the Russian-backed βÄ€peopleβÄôs republicsβÄù in eastern Ukraine in the lead-up to the KremlinβÄôs first invasion in 2014.

At first glance, the effort looks fringe, crude, and unserious. But the episode matters for two reasons: first, because Narva and EstoniaβÄôs border regions have long been recurring targets in Russian influence narratives; and second, because the public reaction to such provocations can sometimes do more to spread them than their original creato
creator: Michael Newton

title: Merger Control in 2026
content:

Below-threshold mergers and call-in rights, evolving theories of harm, an ongoing review of the EU Merger Guidelines and new approaches to remedies βÄ™ it is evident that significant merger control policy changes are underway, as competition authorities and policy makers alike are struggling to adapt the existing merger control framework to the current market realities. While no changes to either the Finnish Competition Act or the EU Merger Regulation (βÄ€EUMRβÄù) are expected in 2026, the Finnish Competition and Consumer Authority (βÄ€FCCAβÄù) has been vocal about the changes to merger control it would like to see. title: Mapping Google's Unmappable City
content:
North Oaks, Minnesota is the only city in the United States that is not on Google Maps Street View. YouTube documentarian Chris Parr, who grew up not too far from North Oaks, set out to change that earlier this year. For a brief few days, he literally put North Oaks on the map. And then it was gone again.

βÄ€ItβÄôs known by Minnesotans as a place where executives and CEOs live,βÄù Parr told 404 Media. βÄ€Famously Walter Mondale is from North Oaks, but also like United Healthcare executives and Target executives.βÄù

title: Slain Iranian OfficialβÄôs Sons Hold $29M in Dubai Property Using Assumed Names
content:
A sanctioned Iranian oil magnate and his brother have used aliases and Caribbean βÄ€golden passportsβÄù to amass a $29 million luxury real estate portfolio in Dubai, property records show.

Hossein Shamkhani and his brother, Abolfazl, are the sons of Ali Shamkhani, a political adviser to IranβÄôs late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. Their father was killed during recent U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran that claimed the lives of Khamenei and other top officials, according to ILNA, a semi-official Iranian news agency.

title: You probably agree with the animals on which bird calls, frog noises and cricket chirps are most attractive βÄ™ new research
content:
Animals do all sorts of things to attract each other as potential mates. Many birds, for example, produce feathers with elaborate color patterns βÄ™ from the iridescent plumage of many hummingbirds to the famously brilliant tail of a peacock. Charles Darwin, an early pioneer in the theory of evolution, saw these colors and concluded that they exist because other birds find them attractive.

But this raised a peculiar question: Why did Darwin himself find these colors beautiful too?

title: Mapped: How climate change affects extreme weather around the world
content:
In the early 2000s, a new field of climate-science research emerged that began to explore the human fingerprint on extreme weather, such as floods, heatwaves, droughts and storms.

Known as βÄ€extreme event attributionβÄù, the field has gained momentum, not only in the science world, but also in the media and public imagination. These studies have the power to link the seemingly abstract concept of climate change with personal and tangible experiences of the weather.

title: Enact: a repository created for practice research
content:
"


Delivered by a team of academic, repository, research data and open standards experts, in co-design with all Enact communities.



The Enact Community includes discipline based academics and researchers and those with specialist skills to enable sharing of practice research - from repository and research data managers to curators, research managers, software developers and more. Each of which represents an intersecting community - all with their own cultures and norms.


Enact will capture and present the content, context and narrative and connections of practice research process and outputs and enable Findable, Accessible, Interoperable and Resuable (FAIR) for this research. It will capture a broad range of contributorship while respecting intellectual property and ethics."

title: Massive Illegal Cigarette Factory Dismantled in Italy
content:
Italian police, acting on a request from the European Public ProsecutorβÄôs Office (EPPO), have dismantled a clandestine cigarette factory in northern Italy capable of producing four million cigarettes a day, authorities said.

During the raid near Verona, which involved more than 70 officers, police arrested 13 people, including 11 Bulgarian and Ukrainian nationals found working inside and the facility's two Italian owners. The suspects face charges of smuggling and trademark counterfeiting.

title: Inside ChinaβÄôs robotics revolution
content:
How close are we to the sci-fi vision of autonomous humanoid robots? I visited 11 companies in five Chinese cities to find out

Chen Liang, the founder of Guchi Robotics, an automation company headquartered in Shanghai, is a tall, heavy-set man in his mid-40s with square-rimmed glasses. His everyday manner is calm and understated, but when he is in his element βÄ™ up close with the technology he builds, or in business meetings discussing the imminent replacement of human workers by robots βÄ™ he wears an exuberant smile that brings to mind an intern on his first day at his dream job. Guchi makes the machines that install wheels, dashboards and windows for many of the top Chinese car brands, including BYD and Nio. He took the name from the Chinese word guzhi, βÄ€steadfast intell

title: The People Behind Open Science: Gerda McNeill Reflects on Her NOAD Journey - OpenAIRE Blog
content:
"After more than fifteen years as Austria's National Open Access Desk (NOAD), Gerda McNeill is stepping into a new role, closing an important chapter in OpenAIRE's community network.

Over the years, Gerda has played a key role in supporting the development of Open Science in Austria, working with national initiatives, connecting institutions and funders, and helping researchers navigate evolving practices around Open Access and research data. Along the way, she has also been an active member of the OpenAIRE community, contributing to working groups, training initiatives, and collaborative activities across Europe.

We spoke with Gerda about how she first became involved with OpenAIRE, what the NOAD role has meant to her over the years, and what makes the OpenAIRE community unique."

Ne ovat kolhiintuneita ja sΟΛΟΛn pieksΟΛmiΟΛ kuvauksia ihmishahmoista, jotka ovat alkaneet kasvaa vieraaseen, erilajiseen muotoon.

Hamari tyΟΕskentelee isompien teosten kanssa ulkona, jolloin sΟΛΟΛn ja vuodenajan vaihtelut antavat puun pintaan oman patinansa. Veistokset syntyvΟΛt luonnostelematta. title: From gym to jawline: What looksmaxxing says about modern masculinity
content:
Young men and teenage boys are learning to see their faces and bodies as projects to measure and optimize.

On social media platforms like Reddit, Instagram and TikTok, jawlines are dissected, cheekbones compared and percevied βÄ€flawsβÄù catalogued. Widely viewed videos and reels help users to rank their faces and identify areas for improvement. They also advise on just how best to bulk up, trim down, make over and become more desirable βÄî and more masculine.

This growing practice of ritualized self-scrutiny, and the litany of βÄ€solutionsβÄù in service of it,
creator: Jillian Sunderland, PhD candidate in Sociology, University of Toronto

title: Valoon tukemalla Malmin Palloseuralla on vΟΛkevΟΛ koillishelsinkilΟΛinen identiteetti
content:
MPS:n identiteetti on vahvasti sidoksissa Malmiin ja sen lΟΛhialueisiin. Seura hallinnoi kahta lΟΛhellΟΛ toisiaan sijaitsevaa kenttΟΛΟΛ, ja kotikenttΟΛ on pysynyt samalla paikalla jo vuodesta 1949. TΟΛmΟΛ pysyvyys on luonut vahvan pohjan yhteisΟΕllisyydelle. βÄùMeillΟΛ on tuhatkunta pelaajaa ja 16 tyΟΕntekijΟΛΟΛ, joista 13 on valmentajia. Olemme ylpeΟΛsti paikallinen seura, joka ei haali pelaajia alueensa ulkopuolelta, vaan haluamme pysyΟΛ jatkossakin nimenomaan oman kylΟΛn jenginΟΛβÄù, Ilvonen kertoo.

Arvovalintoja perheiden arjen helpottamiseksi

MPS:n toiminnassa korostuvat arvovalinnat, jotka nΟΛkyvΟΛt konkreettisesti perheiden arjessa. Koska kentΟΛt sijaitsevat lΟΛhellΟΛ toisiaan ja kaikkea muutakin, suuri osa pelaajista pΟΛΟΛsee harrastuksiin itsen

title: Less Talk, More Security: Cyber Lessons Learned from Munich
content:
Awareness of cyber threats has risen dramatically in recent years. Policymakers, industry leaders, and security practitioners now broadly acknowledge that cybersecurity is a core component of national and economic security. That recognition is an important first step βÄî but awareness alone does not automatically translate into meaningful action.

In many cases, growing urgency has produced a cascade of regulations, reporting requirements, and compliance frameworks without clear measures of success. Too often, bureaucratic processes create the illusion that compliance equals security. Real cybersecurity, however, requires actionable steps, operational capability, and measurable outcomes that demonstrably

Kuvataiteilijan tyΟΕssΟΛ tulee vastaan monenlaisia sopimustilanteita. TΟΛssΟΛ artikkelissa kΟΛymme lΟΛpi, mitΟΛ sopimuksissa kannattaa huomioida tekijΟΛnoikeuksien nΟΛkΟΕkulmasta ja milloin voit jΟΛttΟΛΟΛ sopimisen Kuvaston hoidettavaksi.

Taiteilijana saatat kohdata tyΟΕssΟΛsi monesti sopimuksia, jotka vaihtelevat muodoltaan ja sisΟΛllΟΕltΟΛΟΛn. Sopimus voi koskea esimerkiksi tilausteosta, teoshankintaa, nΟΛyttelyΟΛ tai teoksen kΟΛyttΟΕΟΛ. NΟΛitΟΛ sopimuksia yhdistΟΛΟΛ se, ettΟΛ ne sisΟΛltΟΛvΟΛt varsin usein tekijΟΛnoikeuksia koskevia ehtoja. Siksi sopimukseen ei kannata suhtautua vain muodollisuutena.

title: βÄ‰No to warβÄô: more than a Spanish slogan βÄ™ a universal cry as Europe fails to act
content:
On 27 February, the United States and Israel attacked Iran without the approval of the UN Security Council and in violation of Article 2 of the United Nations Charter. Both governments justified the operation as a βÄ‰preventive strikeβÄô, a concept that does not exist in international law.

The bombings took place just hours after Iran had accepted, in negotiations with the United States mediated by Oman, measures such as not storing enriched uranium. The imminent threat invoked by both powers before launching the attack contradicted the International Atomic Energy Agency, whose inspectors had stated that they had found no evidence of a systematic programme to build nuclear weapons. It is true that Iran had enriched uranium to 60 per cent purity, far above civilian
creator: Hedwig Giusto

title: Trying your best in a second language? HereβÄôs why native speakers seem so rude
content:
Rudeness, whether real or perceived, can deeply affect cooperation, trust, and workplace culture. But judgements of what we consider rude arenβÄôt confined to specific disrespectful words or phrases βÄ™ they are shaped by the listenerβÄôs emotional processing, attention to non-verbal cues, and underlying moral stance.

In multilingual settings this complexity is compounded, as misunderstandings donβÄôt just arise from vocabulary gaps or grammar mistakes. In fact, they often have more to do with ourselves βÄ™ our own emotional and moral judgements of what others say and do βÄ™ than the words being uttered.

If you frequently communicate in your second language, youβÄôll have encountered this regularly. Someone might speak to you calmly, clearly and without a hint of ill will, but still l
creator: Irini Mavrou, Associate professor, Universidad Nebrija; UCL

title: Where did the ancient Greeks and Romans think lightning came from? Hint: not just the gods
content:
Is it any wonder ancient people thought lightning came from the gods? Even today a close lightning strike feels like a terrifying brush with the supernatural.

Some ancient thinkers, however, suspected the gods had nothing to do with it.

They wondered, centuries ahead of their time, if lightning was related somehow to the movement of air and clouds.

A reminder of power and wrath

In the mythology of ancient Greece and Rome, thunder and lightning strikes were the prime weapon of Zeus (the king of the gods, known to the Romans as Jupiter). Reminders of his power and wrath via lightning strikes were everywhere.

The ancient Greek poet Hesiod (who was writing around 700 BCE, about the same time as Homer) described Zeus hurling bolts of lightning and t
creator: Peter Edwell, Associate Professor in Ancient History, Macquarie University

title: New Research Discovers How Child Sexual Abuse Material Perpetrators Use Online Platforms and Technologies
content:
Each year, millions of images and videos depicting child sexual abuse circulate online, causing profound and long-lasting harm to victims and survivors. Despite efforts to tackle this threat, the scale and accessibility of CSAM online continue to pose a major global challenge.

The new report published by Protect Children and Ofcom presents groundbreaking findings from a survey of 20,592 individuals searching for child sexual abuse material online. The research was presented at an event with expert speakers from regulators, law enforcement, and civil society.

title: Inside the fiery, deadly crashes involving the Tesla Cybertruck
content:
Cybertrucks have locked passengers inside and burned so hot theyβÄôve disintegrated driversβÄô bones. VictimsβÄô families blame what they say is the faulty design of a truck Elon Musk calls βÄ‰apocalypse-proofβÄô

When sheriff deputies arrived at the scene of a late-night crash off a desolate Texas road in August 2024, they could see a giant pyre through heavy smoke.

According to police reports detailing the events of that night, the officers tried to approach the vehicle, but the fire burned too intensely. They saw it was a Tesla Cybertruck and couldnβÄôt see anyone inside. So they combed the surrounding area for the driver.

title: The Incredible Shrinking Public Domain: Section 104A βÄ™ Authors Alliance
content:
<html><head></head><body>"As anyone who has ever lived in an old house knows, sometimes additions and renovations are great. Sometimes they are good ideas but poorly executed. And sometimes they are just bad ideas from the start. That last category is where IβÄôd put 104A.

Section 104A is responsible for one of the greatest contractions of the public domain in history. ItβÄôs the first provision weβÄôve covered in this series that wasnβÄôt part of the original 1976 Act βÄî it was a later addition that fundamentally changed the landscape of what works could be freely used in the United States. Section 104A was first added to Title 17 in December of 1993 as part of a series of changes related to the North American Free Trade Agreement (originally covering only motion pictures produced in NAFTA countries). βÄùJo lapsuudessa oli selviΟΕ, ettΟΛ harakan nauru tietΟΛΟΛ epΟΛonnea ja leppΟΛkerttu onnea. Minua kiinnosti, miksi. Olen aina ajatellut, ettΟΛ uskomusten tΟΛytyy perustua johonkinβÄù, Piia Kaskinen kertoo.

βÄùEnnen uskottiin, ettΟΛ ihmisen onni riippui siitΟΛ, kuinka hΟΛn kohteli muita eliΟΕitΟΛ. NykyΟΛΟΛn tiedetΟΛΟΛn, ettΟΛ nΟΛin on: tapa, jolla kohtelemme muita lajeja, mΟΛΟΛrittΟΛΟΛ myΟΕs oman hyvinvointimme. title: Sharing JujuyβÄôs Andean Carnival with the world
content:
The Andean Carnival in the Province of Jujuy, Argentina, is one of the most significant cultural celebrations in the countryβÄôs northern region. Music, dance, masks, talco (talcum powder traditionally thrown during carnival celebrations), and comparsas (organized carnival groups) transform streets into spaces of gathering where community, memory, and territory intertwine. These celebrations are part of deeply rooted Andean traditions, passed down from generation to generation and experienced as collective moments of gratitude, abundance, and the celebration of life.

title: Five lessons from three years of risk assessments under the Digital Services Act
content:
Under the Digital Services Act (DSA), Big Tech platforms are required to annually assess systemic risks tied to their services and implement measures to mitigate them. EDRi member, the European Center for Not-for-Profit Law (ECNL) analysed the first three rounds of these risk assessments, spanning from 2023 to 2025, and identified five major gaps. In their current form, these assessments are unlikely to provide meaningful transparency or accountability for decisions affecting millions of internet users, raising fundamental questions about their usefulness and future direction.

The post Five lessons from three years of risk assessments under the Digital Services Ac

title: US dangles steel concessions ahead of key EU votes
content:
BRUSSELS βÄî The Trump administration has reassured the EUβÄôs top trade lawmaker that it plans to shorten a list of items containing steel that are subject to high U.S. tariffs, in a concession that could finally persuade the European Parliament to back last yearβÄôs transatlantic trade deal.

The offer came in a call between U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer with Bernd Lange, the chair of the European ParliamentβÄôs Trade Committee. It has helped win the support of LangeβÄôs fellow socialists, enabling a key committee vote to go ahead on Thursday.

But the fix is not yet fully in, with caucus leaders still to debate exactly when to schedule a final plenary vote

The tech giantβÄôs βÄ€lack of proper detailed security documentationβÄù left reviewers with a βÄ€lack of confidence in assessing the systemβÄôs overall security posture,βÄù according to an internal government report reviewed by ProPublica.

title: Project Hail Mary is packed with hard science. An astrophysicist breaks it down
content:
As an astrophysicist, my world revolves around the wonders of space and the mysteries of the universe. This means I can be a tough critic of science fiction books and films that explore these topics.

But when I walked out of a recent preview screening of the film adaptation of Andy WeirβÄôs 2021 science fiction novel Project Hail Mary, I had tears of joy in my eyes. The filmmakers had done justice not just to the original story, but also to the science at the heart of it.

The story revolves around Ryland Grace, played by Ryan Gosling, who awakes from a coma with no memory and no idea why heβÄôs on a space ship 11.9 light years away from Earth. As his memories slowly start to return, the truth becomes clear. The Sun is dying, and he is our only saving grace.

Tutkimus tarjoaa ainutlaatuista ja uutta tietoa siitΟΛ, miten lapsiin kohdistuvaa seksuaalivΟΛkivaltaa todistavaa kuvamateriaalia (CSAM) kΟΛyttΟΛvΟΛt rikoksentekijΟΛt toimivat digitaalisilla alustoilla ja millaista teknologiaa he hyΟΕdyntΟΛvΟΛt rikolliseen toimintaan.

Tutkimustulokset koostuvat yli 20 000 rikoksentekijΟΛn vastauksista

Tutkimus perustuu kyselyyn, johon osallistui 20 592 henkilΟΕΟΛ, jotka etsivΟΛt lapsiin kohdistuvaa seksuaalivΟΛkivaltaa todistavaa kuvamateriaalia verkossa. Aineisto tarjoaa harvinaisen nΟΛkymΟΛn siihen, miten laittoman kuvamateriaalin kΟΛyttΟΛjΟΛt hyΟΕdyntΟΛvΟΛt digitaalisia alustoja ja teknologioita toiminnassaan.

65 prosenttia vastaajista kertoi nΟΛhneensΟΛ pornografiaa ja 59 prosenttia lapsiin kohdistuvaa seksuaalivΟΛkivaltaa

title: Library and Archives 101: AI and the False Promise of Control βÄ™ Authors Alliance
content: "This is the first in a series of essays about the choices that knowledge institutions face as AI reshapes how their collections and resources are used. The people on various sides of these debates are, for the most part, deeply committed to access to knowledge βÄî the disagreement is about means. Some believe that the best way to protect access to knowledge is to impose new conditions on how AI can use it and for what purposes. I think that approach is more likely to undermine access than to save it. This post in particular is about the risk of libraries and archives reimagining themselves as gatekeepers of AI-era access. To help contain these essays, we will be posting them as a series titled βÄ€AI & Access to Knowledge.βÄù "

content:
Highlife recently turned 100! To commemorate this centenary, the Wikimedia Ghana User Group (WMGH) has launched the Wikipedia Highlife @ 100 Project, an initiative aimed at documenting and preserving the history of Highlife music online.

Recognized by UNESCO on its Intangible Cultural Heritage list, Highlife has long served as a medium of storytelling, and over the decades, has inspired generations of musicians both in Ghana and across the diaspora.

title: Artificial Insecurity: how AI tools compromise confidentiality
content:
Whatever you think about the promises or perils of AI, itβÄôs becoming increasingly impossible to ignore that these tools are beset by glaring security vulnerabilities. From exposing user data to facilitating hacks, from undermining information integrity to creating supply chain vulnerabilities, AI tools are underpinned, and undermined, by dodgy security practices. As weβÄôll explore in this series, this has grave consequences for the confidentiality of our data, for information integrity, and for access to and availability of systems βÄî all problems that a human rights-respecting approach can help solve.

title: Elon MuskβÄôs X cooperates with β²§120M EU fine
content:
BRUSSELS βÄî Elon MuskβÄôs X has met its deadline for the β²§120 million fine issued by the EU in December, a European Commission spokesperson confirmed.

The cooperation with the EU comes as X continues a legal challenge against the decision.

Under the ruling announced in December, X had a deadline this month to pay the fine and to offer remedies on the design of blue checkmarks for verified accounts on its service. βÄ€Both of them have been done,βÄù Commission spokesperson Thomas Regnier said.

Meeting the deadline means X either paying the fine or offering a financial guarantee that it will do so should its appeal against the fine fail.

title: An Open Letter to the Inspectors General Community
content:
Dear current and former members of the inspectors general community,

Last year, in a highly unusual move, President Donald Trump fired more than 18 inspectors general without specific justifications, as the law requires, and replaced several of them with political loyalists. Over the past weeks, we have spoken with dozens of people who have experience in this field. They have given us important context on how these offices work. Some have expressed concerns that these new federal government watchdogs may be unable to independently carry out their critical oversight duties.

We recognize the longstanding reluctance of inspectors general and their staffs to speak with the media. But this is an extraordinary moment. As
creator: Colleen.Barry@propublica.org

title: En halua tietΟΛΟΛ, kuka Banksy on
content:
Johanna Kohvakka

SiinΟΛ on jotain poikkeuksellista, ettΟΛ 2000-luvun lΟΛpivalaistussa maailmassa jokin asia onnistuu pysymΟΛΟΛn tuntemattomana. Ei siksi, ettei sitΟΛ olisi yritetty selvittΟΛΟΛ, vaan siksi, ettΟΛ se ei ole koskaan ollut olennaista.

Banksy on ilmiΟΕ, joka toimii juuri siksi, ettΟΛ tekijΟΛ puuttuu. Teokset ilmestyvΟΛt kuin luonnonilmiΟΕt: seinille, konfliktialueille, keskelle merkityksellistΟΛ hetkeΟΛ. Ilman nimeΟΛ ne eivΟΛt ole osa henkilΟΕkulttia vaan osa keskustelua.

Banksyn tyΟΕt kritisoivat valtaa, kulutuskulttuuria ja instituutioita erityisellΟΛ tavalla juuri siksi, ettΟΛ ne eivΟΛt ole sidottuja yksilΟΕn brΟΛndiin. Kun tekijΟΛ poistetaan yhtΟΛlΟΕstΟΛ, jΟΛljelle jΟΛΟΛ viesti. Se on harvinaista ajassa, jossa lΟΛhes kaikki merkityksellinen kytkeytyy henkilΟΕih

The agreement enables participating Norwegian institutions to access discounts on Article Processing Charges (APCs) via MDPIβÄôs Institutional Open Access Program (IOAP), supporting wider dissemination of publicly funded research.

title: FranceβÄôs Ex-President Sarkozy Appeals Conviction in Libya Corruption Case
content:
Anti-corruption watchdogs are closely monitoring the historic appeals trial of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy this week, pointing to his conviction for soliciting illegal campaign cash from the Libyan dictator Muammar Gaddafi as a stark reminder that top-level political graft is never a victimless crime.

The 71-year-old former president is challenging a September ruling that sentenced him to five years in prison, a 100,000-euro (about $117,000) fine and a five-year ban from public office. He was acquitted on several other counts due to insufficient evidence, and ultimately spent only 20 days behind bars before an appeals court ordered his release.

title: Hundreds of hungry mosquitoes, a student volunteer and a mesh suit helped us figure out how these deadly insects reach their targets
content:
βÄ€Four minutes is too long.βÄù

ThatβÄôs the note undergraduate Chris Zuo sent me along with photos of countless mosquito bites on his bare skin. This full-body massacre wasnβÄôt the result of a camping trip gone awry. HeβÄôd spent that limited amount of time in a room with 100 hungry mosquitoes while wearing nothing but a mesh suit we thought would have protected him.

Thus began our three-year journey trying to understand the behavior of a deceivingly simple insect, the mosquito. It may sound like a professorβÄôs sadistic plan, but, really, we did everything by the book. Our universityβÄôs institutional review board approved our procedures, making sure Chris was safe and not coerced in any way. The mosquitoes were disease-free an

title: Human vision: what we actually see βÄ™ and donβÄôt see βÄ™ tells us a lot about consciousness
content:
What can you see right now? This might seem like a silly question, but what enters your consciousness is not the whole story when it comes to vision. A great deal of visual processing in the brain goes on well below our conscious awareness.

Some studies have probed the unconscious depths of vision. One source of evidence comes from the neurological condition known as blindsight, which is caused by damage to areas of the brain involved in processing visual information. People with blindsight report that they are unable to see, either entirely or in a portion of their visual field. However, when asked to guess what is there, they can often do so wit

title: The eLife Model: Comparing eLife Assessments across revisions | Inside eLife | eLife
content:
<html><head></head><body>When an article is first published as a Reviewed Preprint in eLife, it includes an eLife Assessment written by the editors and reviewers. This assessment uses a common vocabulary to describe the significance of the findings and the strength of the evidence reported in the article. This approach promotes the assessment of research at the article level, rather than relying on journal names or journal-level metrics.

Following publication of the first version of a Reviewed Preprint, authors can choose to publish their article as a regular journal article (Version of Record; VOR) and mark the end of the process. However, many authors revise their work before requesting a VOR. When authors revise, the editors and reviewers reassess the work and consider updates to the eLife </body></html>

title: His Majesty King Charles III and FinlandβÄôs President Alexander Stubb explore Finnish bioeconomy innovations in London
content:
The New Wood: Building a Bio-Based Future exhibition showcases Finnish wood-based innovations that offer alternatives to fossil-based materials across packaging, textiles, chemicals, and even designer bathtubs.

Organised by the Finnish Forest Association, the New Wood exhibition was invited to London by the Circular Bioeconomy Alliance (CBA), whose goal is to accelerate the global transition to a climate-neutral bioeconomy based on the circular economy. The CBA was founded by The King, a long-time advocate of the environment and sustainable development.

Marc PalahΟ≠, CEO of the Circular Bioeconomy Alliance, stated that bioeconomy solutions need visibility and concrete examples.

title: Into the unknown: Tales of exploration and survival
content:
From the vast reaches of outer space to the depths of the Mariana Trench, the LibraryβÄôs collections chronicle some of the Western worldβÄôs greatest voyages of discovery and exploration, often yearslong epics that illuminated the mysteries of the world and of ourselves.

These are journeys that crossed time and space, shattering the old realms of myth and superstition and revealing the known world, a place of maps and charts and taxonomic tables. Giants and dragons did not exist, it turned out, but a whole new universe filled with strange and wonderful things did.

title: The Digital Omnibus: A step back from the brink, but the risks remain
content:
A first Council compromise on the Digital Omnibus removes several of the most dangerous changes that were originally proposed to the GDPR and the ePrivacy Directive. This is a welcome development, however, important risks remain. Some amendments could still weaken safeguards in practice, while new provisions on AI development and access to terminal equipment remain unresolved. Simplification should strengthen rights, not dilute them. The safest way to protect EuropeβÄôs digital rulebook is to reject the Digital Omnibus entirely.

The post The Digital Omnibus: A step back from the brink, but the risks remain appeared first on European Digital Rights
creator: EDRi
sourceDescription: Protecting digital rights in Europe.

title: Agents of Chaos: Unpacking the Actions of Border Patrol Agents Across the US
content:
This investigation is part of a collaboration between Bellingcat, Evident Media and CalMatters. You can watch EvidentβÄôs investigative video here, and read CalMattersβÄô report here.

In early January 2025, a gardener named Ernesto Campos was pulled over by Border Patrol agents in the city of Bakersfield, California.

The agents were a long way from home: Bakersfield is over 240 miles (386km) from the US border with Mexico.

They were there as part of Operation Return to Sender, a Border Patrol surge in the city that acted as a portent of what was to come across the US in 2025.
creator: Bellingcat Investigation Team

title: Oil Regulators Found Hundreds of Wells Violating Oklahoma Rules. Then They Ignored Their Findings.
content:
Five years ago, Oklahoma oil regulators took on a project with an impressive name: the Source of Truth. State officials wanted a comprehensive database capturing all vital information about the more than 11,000 wells in Oklahoma that shoot the toxic byproduct of oil production back underground.

IβÄôd heard about this project from several people during the 18 months I had spent reporting on the growing number of cases where oilfield wastewater blasted out of old wells, known as purges, after being injected underground at high pressures. State employees also referenced the project in internal communications that I received after filing nearly a dozen public records requests to the Oklahoma Corporation Commission, which regulates the oil and gas industry.

title: Vill du ha ett responssamtal med oss?

Nu kan du boka en tid fΟΕr ett responssamtal.

GΟΞ till bokningssystemet.

Alla tider i systemet ΟΛr finsk tid (UTC +2). Om du inte bor i Finland och anvΟΛnder en digital kalender som automatiskt hΟΛmtar bokningen frΟΞn din mejl ska du kontrollera att tidpunkten ΟΛr korrekt noterad i din kalender.

title: Politiikkasuositus: Matkailun kiihdyttΟΛmΟΛn asumisen kriisin hallinta RovaniemellΟΛ
content:
Rovaniemen kaupungin asuntomarkkinat ovat matkailun myΟΕtΟΛ kriisiytyneet. LisΟΛΟΛntynyt matkailu tuo taloudellista hyΟΕtyΟΛ, mutta kiristΟΛΟΛ samalla asuntotilannetta. Lapin yliopiston ja Aalto-yliopiston tutkijoiden julkaisema politiikkasuositus esittelee toimenpiteitΟΛ kriisin ratkaisemiseksi.

Rovaniemen kaupunki tasapainoilee matkailun tuoman hyΟΕdyn ja sen aiheuttaman kuormituksen vΟΛlillΟΛ. Kaupungin asuntomarkkinoilla on puute erityisesti kohtuuhintaisista asunnoista. Lyhytaikainen vuokraus vie asuntoja pitkΟΛaikaisilta asukkailta, nostaa vuokria ja lisΟΛΟΛ epΟΛvarmuutta kohtuuhintaista kotia etsiville.
